{{Short description|none}}
{{Lead rewrite|date=August 2023}}
{{Globalize|reason=This article lacks significant information about non-European ethnicities|date=April 2016}}
The various [[ethnolinguistic groups]] found in the [[Caucasus]], [[Central Asia]], [[Europe]], the [[Middle East]], [[North Africa]] and/or [[South Asia]] demonstrate differing rates of particular [[Y-DNA]] [[haplogroup]]s.

In the table below, the first two columns identify [[ethnolinguistic group]]s. Subsequent columns represent the [[sample size]] (''n'') of the study or studies cited, and the [[percentage]] of each haplogroup found in that particular sample.

(Data from studies conducted before 2004 may be inaccurate or a broad estimate, due to [[Conversion table for Y chromosome haplogroups|obsolete haplogroup naming systems]] – e.g. the former ''Haplogroup 2'' included members of the relatively unrelated haplogroups known later as [[haplogroup G (Y-DNA)|Haplogroup G]] and [[haplogroup IJ|macrohaplogroup IJ]] [which comprises haplogroups I and J].)

{| class="wikitable sortable" style="text-align:center; font-size: 84%"
! Population
! Language family<ref name="IE=Indo-European">IE=Indo-European</ref>
! ''n''<ref name="ReferenceA">First column gives the amount of total Sample Size studied</ref> !! [[Haplogroup R1b (Y-DNA)|R1b]]<ref name="ReferenceB">Second column gives the Percentage of the particular haplogroup among the Sample Size</ref>
! ''n'' !! [[Haplogroup R1a (Y-DNA)|R1a]]
! ''n'' !! [[Haplogroup I (Y-DNA)|I]]
! ''n'' !! [[Haplogroup E1b1b (Y-DNA)|E1b1b]]
! ''n'' !! [[Haplogroup E1b1a (Y-DNA)|E1b1a]]
! ''n'' !! [[Haplogroup J (Y-DNA)|J]]
! ''n'' !! [[Haplogroup G (Y-DNA)|G]]
! ''n'' !! [[Haplogroup N (Y-DNA)|N]]
! ''n'' !! [[Haplogroup T (Y-DNA)|T]]
! ''n'' !! [[Haplogroup L (Y-DNA)|L]]
! ''n'' !! [[Haplogroup H (Y-DNA)|H]]
|-
! [[Cyprus|Cypriots]]
| IE (Greek)
| 45 || {{nts|9.0}}<ref name="Rosser2000"/>
| 45 || {{nts|2.0}}<ref name="Rosser2000"/>
| ||
| 45 || {{nts|27.0}}<ref name="Rosser2000"/>
| ||
| ||
| ||
| ||
| ||
| ||
| ||
|-
! [[Greeks]]
| IE (Greek)
| 92 || {{nts|19.6}}<ref name="Battaglia2008">{{cite journal |last1=Battaglia |first1=Vincenza |last2=Fornarino |first2=Simona |last3=Al-Zahery |first3=Nadia |last4=Olivieri |first4=Anna |last5=Pala |first5=Maria |last6=Myres |first6=Natalie M |last7=King |first7=Roy J |last8=Rootsi |first8=Siiri |last9=Marjanovic |first9=Damir |last10=Primorac |first10=Dragan |last11=Hadziselimovic |first11=Rifat |last12=Vidovic |first12=Stojko |last13=Drobnic |first13=Katia |last14=Durmishi |first14=Naser |last15=Torroni |first15=Antonio |last16=Santachiara-Benerecetti |first16=A Silvana |last17=Underhill |first17=Peter A |last18=Semino |first18=Ornella |display-authors=8 |date=24 December 2008 |title=Y-chromosomal evidence of the cultural diffusion of agriculture in southeast Europe |journal=European Journal of Human Genetics |url=http://www.draganprimorac.com/wp-content/uploads/2010/05/Battaglia.pdf |volume=17 |issue=6 |pages=820–30 |doi=10.1038/ejhg.2008.249 |pmc=2947100 |pmid=19107149}}</ref>
| 92 || {{nts|16.3}}<ref name="Battaglia2008"/>
| 92 || {{nts|9.8}}<ref name="Battaglia2008"/>
| 92 || {{nts|21.8}}<ref name="Battaglia2008"/>
| ||
| 92 || {{nts|22.9}}<ref name="Battaglia2008"/>
| 92 || {{nts|3.3}}<ref name="Battaglia2008"/>
| ||
| 92 || {{nts|4.3}}<ref name="Battaglia2008"/>
| 92 || {{nts|1.1}}<ref name="Battaglia2008"/>
| ||
|-
! [[Greeks]]
| IE (Greek)
| 77 || {{nts|11.7}}<br>9/77<ref name="Firasat2007"/>
| 77 || {{nts|15.6}}<br>12/77<ref name="Firasat2007"/>
| 77 || {{nts|19.5}}<br>15/77<ref name="Firasat2007"/>
| 77 || {{nts|20.8}}<br>16/77<ref name="Firasat2007"/>
| 77 || 
| 77 || {{nts|16.9}}<br>13/77<ref name="Firasat2007"/>
| 77 || {{nts|9.1}}<br>7/77<ref name="Firasat2007"/>
| 77 || {{nts|0.0}}<ref name="Firasat2007"/>
| 77 || {{nts|0.0}}<ref name="Firasat2007"/>
| 77 || {{nts|6.49}}<br>others<br>5/77
| ||
|-
! Greeks
| IE (Greek)
| 118 || {{nts|22.8}}<ref name="Semino2000"/><ref name="Helgason2000">{{cite journal |last1=Helgason |first1=Agnar |last2=Sigureth Ardóttir |first2=S |last3=Nicholson |first3=J |last4=Sykes |first4=B |last5=Hill |first5=E. W. |last6=Bradley |first6=D. G. |last7=Bosnes |first7=V |last8=Gulcher |first8=J. R. |last9=Ward |first9=R |last10=Stefánsson |first10=K |display-authors=etal |date=2000 |title=Estimating Scandinavian and Gaelic Ancestry in the Male Settlers of Iceland |url=http://www.ajhg.org/AJHG/abstract/S0002-9297(07)63256-X |journal=Am. J. Hum. Genet. |volume=67 |issue=3 |pages=697–717 |pmc=1287529 |pmid=10931763 |doi=10.1086/303046 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20081011075349/http://www.ajhg.org/AJHG/abstract/S0002-9297(07)63256-X |archive-date=2008-10-11 |url-status=dead}}</ref>
| 118 || {{nts|8.3}}<ref name="Semino2000"/><ref name="Helgason2000"/>
| 261 || {{nts|13.8}}<ref name="Rootsi2004"/>
| 84 || {{nts|23.8}}<ref name="Semino2004"/>
| ||
| 92 || {{nts|6.5}}<ref name="Semino2004"/>
| ||
| ||
| ||
| ||
| ||
|-
! Greeks
| IE (Greek)
| 171 || {{nts|13.5}}<br>23/171<ref name="King et al. 2008">{{cite journal | last1 = King | first1 = R. J. | last2 = Özcan | first2 = S. S. | last3 = Carter | first3 = T. | last4 = Kalfoğlu | first4 = E. | last5 = Atasoy | first5 = S. | last6 = Triantaphyllidis | first6 = C. | last7 = Kouvatsi | first7 = A. | last8 = Lin | first8 = A. A. | last9 = Chow | first9 = C-E. T. | last10 = Zhivotovsky | first10 = L. A. | last11 = Michalodimitrakis | first11 = M. | last12 = Underhill | first12 = P. A. | year = 2008 | title = Differential Y-chromosome Anatolian Influences on the Greek and Cretan Neolithic | journal = Annals of Human Genetics | volume = 72 | issue = 2| pages = 205–214 | doi = 10.1111/j.1469-1809.2007.00414.x | pmid = 18269686 | s2cid = 22406638 }}</ref>
| 171 || {{nts|11.1}}<br>19/171
| 171 || {{nts|15.8}}<br>27/171
| 171 || {{nts|31.6}}<br>54/171
| ||
| 171 || {{nts|19.9}}<br>34/171
| 171 || {{nts|4.7}}<br>8/171
| ||
| 171 || {{nts|1.8}}<br>3/171
| ||
| ||
|-
! Greeks ([[Crete]])
| IE (Greek)
| 193 || {{nts|17.0}}<br>33/193<ref name="King et al. 2008"/>
| 193 || {{nts|8.8}}<br>17/193
| 193 || {{nts|13.0}}<br>25/193
| 193 || {{nts|8.8}}<br>17/193
| ||
| 193 || {{nts|38.9}}<br>75/193
| 193 || {{nts|10.9}}<br>21/193
| ||
| 193 || {{nts|2.1}}<br>4/193
| ||
| ||
|-
! Greeks ([[Crete]])
| IE (Greek)
| '''171+<br>193=<br>364''' || '''{{nts|15.38}}<br>56/364'''<ref name="King et al. 2008"/>
| '''364''' || '''{{nts|9.89}}<br>36/364'''
| '''364''' || '''{{nts|14.29}}<br>52/364'''
| '''364''' || '''{{nts|19.51}}<br>71/364'''
| ||
| '''364''' || '''{{nts|29.95}}<br>109/364'''
| '''364''' || '''{{nts|7.97}}<br>29/364'''
| ||
| '''364''' || '''{{nts|1.92}}<br>7/364'''
| ||
| ||
|-
! Greeks (Peloponnese)
| IE (Greek)
| ||
| ||
| ||
| 36 || {{nts|47.44}}<br>17/36<ref name="Semino2004"/>
| ||
| 36 || {{nts|13.89}}<br>5/36<ref name="Semino2004"/>
| ||
| ||
| ||
| ||
| ||
|-
! Greeks ([[Thrace]])
| IE (Greek)
| 41 || {{nts|12.2}}<ref name="Bosch2006"/>
| 41 || {{nts|22.0}}<ref name="Bosch2006"/>
| 41 || {{nts|19.5}}<ref name="Bosch2006"/>
| 41 || {{nts|19.5}}<ref name="Bosch2006"/>
| 41 || {{nts|0.0}}<ref name="Bosch2006"/>
| 41 || {{nts|19.5}}<ref name="Bosch2006"/>
| 41 || {{nts|4.9}}<ref name="Bosch2006"/>
| ||
| ||
| ||
| ||
|-
! Greeks (North)
| IE (Greek)
| 96 || {{nts|14.6}}=<ref name="Zalloua2008"/><br>14/96
| 96 || {{nts|18.8}}<ref name="Zalloua2008"/><br>18/96
| 96 || {{nts|12.5}}<ref name="Zalloua2008"/><br>12/96
| 96 || {{nts|35.4}}<ref name="Zalloua2008"/><br>34/96
| ||
| ||
| 96 || {{nts|5.2}}<ref name="Zalloua2008"/><br>5/96
| ||
| 96 || {{nts|2.1}}<ref name="Zalloua2008"/><br>2/96
| 96 || {{nts|1.0}}<ref name="Zalloua2008"/><br>1/96
| ||
|-
! Greeks (South)
| IE (Greek)
| 46 || {{nts|19.6}}<ref name="Zalloua2008"/><br>9/46
| 46 || {{nts|2.2}}<ref name="Zalloua2008"/><br>1/46
| 46 || {{nts|23.9}}<ref name="Zalloua2008"/><br>11/46
| 46 || {{nts|43.5}}<ref name="Zalloua2008"/><br>20/46
| ||
| ||
| 46 || {{nts|6.5}}<ref name="Zalloua2008"/><br>3/46
| ||
| 46 || {{nts|2.2}}<ref name="Zalloua2008"/><br>1/46
| 46 || {{nts|0.0}}<ref name="Zalloua2008"/>
| ||
|-
! Greeks (North) + (South)
| IE (Greek)
| '''96+<br>46=<br>142''' || '''{{nts|16.2}}<ref name="Zalloua2008"/><br>23/142'''
| '''96+<br>46=<br>142''' || '''{{nts|13.4}}<ref name="Zalloua2008"/><br>19/142'''
| '''96+<br>46=<br>142''' || '''{{nts|16.2}}<ref name="Zalloua2008"/><br>23/142'''
| '''96+<br>46=<br>142''' || '''{{nts|38}}<ref name="Zalloua2008"/><br>54/142'''
| ||
| ||
| '''96+<br>46=<br>142''' || '''{{nts|5.6}}<ref name="Zalloua2008"/><br>8/142'''
| ||
| '''96+<br>46=<br>142''' || '''{{nts|2.1}}<ref name="Zalloua2008"/><br>3/142'''
| '''96+<br>46=<br>142''' || '''{{nts|0.7}}<ref name="Zalloua2008"/><br>1/142'''
| ||
|-
! Population
! Language family<ref name="IE=Indo-European"/>
! ''n''<ref name="ReferenceA"/> !! [[Haplogroup R1b (Y-DNA)|R1b]]<ref name="ReferenceB"/>
! ''n'' !! [[Haplogroup R1a (Y-DNA)|R1a]]
! ''n'' !! [[Haplogroup I (Y-DNA)|I]]
! ''n'' !! [[Haplogroup E1b1b (Y-DNA)|E1b1b]]
! ''n'' !! [[Haplogroup E1b1a (Y-DNA)|E1b1a]]
! ''n'' !! [[Haplogroup J (Y-DNA)|J]]
! ''n'' !! [[Haplogroup G (Y-DNA)|G]]
! ''n'' !! [[Haplogroup N (Y-DNA)|N]]
! ''n'' !! [[Haplogroup T (Y-DNA)|T]]
! ''n'' !! [[Haplogroup L (Y-DNA)|L]]
! ''n'' !! [[Haplogroup H (Y-DNA)|H]]
|}

{{Expand list|date=August 2008}}

{{Y-DNA}}

==See also==
{{Col-begin}}
{{Col-2}}
* '''[[Genetics]]'''
** [[Human mitochondrial DNA haplogroup]]s
** [[Human genome]]
** [[Genetic genealogy]]
** [[Genealogical DNA test]]ing
** [[Race and genetics]]
* '''[[Genetic history]]'''
* [[Timeline of human evolution]]
** [[Recent African origin of modern humans]]
** [[Genetic history of the British Isles]]
** [[Genetic history of the Middle East]]
** [[Genetics and archaeogenetics of South Asia]]
** [[Genetic history of Europe]]
** [[Genetic history of Italy]]
** [[Genetic history of indigenous peoples of the Americas]]
** [[Genetic studies on Jews]]
{{Col-2}}
* '''Lists of Y-DNA haplogroups according to their distribution'''
** [[List of haplogroups of historical and famous figures]]
* [[Y-chromosome haplogroups in populations of the world]]
** [[Y-DNA haplogroups in populations of Europe]]
** [[Y-DNA haplogroups in populations of the Near East]]
** [[Y-DNA haplogroups in populations of North Africa]]
** [[Y-DNA haplogroups in populations of South Asia]]
** [[Y-DNA haplogroups in populations of Central and North Asia]]
** [[Y-DNA haplogroups in populations of East and Southeast Asia]]
** [[Y-DNA haplogroups in populations of Sub-Saharan Africa]]
** [[Y-DNA haplogroups in populations of Oceania]]
** [[Y-DNA haplogroups in indigenous peoples of the Americas]]
** [[Haplogroup G (Y-DNA) by country]]
{{Portal|Evolutionary biology}}
{{Col-end}}

==References==
{{Reflist|2}}

==External links==
* [https://web.archive.org/web/20040728005528/http://www.scs.uiuc.edu/~mcdonald/WorldHaplogroupsMaps.pdf World Haplogroups Maps]
* [https://web.archive.org/web/20070303065549/http://hpgl.stanford.edu/publications/AJHG_2004_v75_Semino.pdf Phylogeography of Y-Chromosome Haplogroup I Reveals Distinct Domains of Prehistoric Gene Flow in Europe]
* [http://download.ajhg.org/AJHG/pdf/PIIS0002929707643651.pdf Phylogeographic Analysis of Haplogroup E3b (E-M215) Y Chromosomes Reveals Multiple Migratory Events Within and Out Of Africa]{{Dead link|date=April 2019 |bot=InternetArchiveBot |fix-attempted=yes }}
* [https://web.archive.org/web/20140606011243/https://www.familytreedna.com/pdf/Derenko2007.pdf Y-chromosome haplogroup N dispersals from south Siberia to Europe]
* [http://www.ethnoancestry.com/index_files/index_data/Haplogroup_R2_Manoukian.pdf A Synthesis of Haplogroup R2]
* [https://web.archive.org/web/20100216062827/http://mbe.oxfordjournals.org/cgi/reprint/msi185v1.pdf High-Resolution Phylogenetic Analysis of Southeastern Europe (SEE) Traces Major Episodes of Paternal Gene Flow Among Slavic Populations]
* [http://www.pnas.org/cgi/reprint/103/4/843.pdf A prehistory of Indian Y chromosomes: Evaluating demic diffusion scenarios] - Appears to be Indian nationalistic declined ...
* [https://web.archive.org/web/20080514120525/http://hpgl.stanford.edu/publications/AJHG_2006_v78_p202-221.pdf Polarity and Temporality of High-Resolution Y-Chromosome Distributions in India Identify Both Indigenous and Exogenous Expansions and Reveal Minor Genetic Influence of Central Asian Pastoralists]
* [https://web.archive.org/web/20071110192734/http://www.familytreedna.com/pdf/caucasus.pdf Mitochondrial DNA and Y-Chromosome Variation in the Caucasus]
* [https://web.archive.org/web/20070205221918/http://www.anrb.ru/molgen/YunusbaevBB.pdf YunusbaevBB]
* [http://www.dnaheritage.com/ysnptree.asp Map and tree based upon the current YCC 2003 tree] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20060218111549/http://dnaheritage.com/ysnptree.asp |date=2006-02-18 }}
* [http://file.oboz.ua/files/vf47721028aea3a_20071226102616.pdf]{{Dead link|date=April 2019 |bot=InternetArchiveBot |fix-attempted=yes }}
* [https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC1181996/ Frequencies of Haplogroup I and its Subhaplogroups]

{{Y-chromosome haplogroups by population}}
{{human genetics}}

[[Category:Human Y-DNA haplogroups|*]]
[[Category:Ethnicity]]
[[Category:Genetic genealogy]]
[[Category:Race (human categorization)]]
[[Category:Genetics-related lists]][[Category:Oldest pages before the refresh]]
[[Category:Oldest pages before the refresh]]
[[Category:Pages]]
[[Category:Git synced pages]]
